3. If direct HTTPS fails, configure proxy: system node autosupport modify -node * -proxy-url http://<proxy>:<port>
4. Test HTTPS delivery: system node autosupport invoke -node <node> -type test
5. Verify delivery: system node autosupport history show -node <node> -fields destination,status
6. Ensure DNS is configured for hostname resolution: dns show
support_case_note: No support case required.

name: DNS Not Configured
description: Detects clusters where DNS is not configured. DNS is essential for hostname resolution required by AutoSupport, Active Directory integration, LDAP authentication, SnapMirror peering, and general management operations.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, dns_domains, management_ip FROM ontap_clusters WHERE system_id = ? AND (dns_domains IS NULL OR dns_domains = '' OR dns_domains = '[]')
condition: Cluster dns_domains is NULL, empty, or empty array
impact: AutoSupport HTTPS delivery will fail (cannot resolve support.netapp.com). Active Directory authentication for CIFS will not work. LDAP authentication will fail. SnapMirror using hostnames will fail. NTP using hostnames will fail.
remediation_steps:
1. Check current DNS configuration: dns show -vserver <admin_svm>
2. Configure DNS for the admin SVM: dns create -vserver <admin_svm> -domains <domain1,domain2> -name-servers <dns1,dns2>
3. Configure DNS for data SVMs: dns create -vserver <data_svm> -domains <domain> -name-servers <dns1,dns2>
4. Verify DNS resolution: dns check -vserver <svm> -name-server <dns_ip>
5. Test hostname resolution: network ping -node <node> -destination <hostname>
6. Ensure at least 2 DNS servers are configured for redundancy
support_case_note: No support case required.
kb_articles: KB-1015500: DNS configuration best practices for ONTAP

name: NTP Not Configured
description: Detects clusters where NTP (Network Time Protocol) is not configured. Accurate time synchronization is critical for log correlation, Kerberos authentication, SnapMirror scheduling, certificate validation, and forensic analysis.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, ntp_servers, management_ip FROM ontap_clusters WHERE system_id = ? AND (ntp_servers IS NULL OR ntp_servers = '' OR ntp_servers = '[]')
condition: Cluster ntp_servers is NULL, empty, or empty array
impact: Clock drift causes Kerberos authentication failures. Log timestamps are unreliable for forensic analysis. Certificate validation may fail due to time mismatch. SnapMirror schedules may execute at unexpected times. Active Directory authentication will break with >5 minute clock skew.
remediation_steps:
1. Check current NTP configuration: cluster time-service ntp server show
2. Add NTP servers: cluster time-service ntp server create -server <ntp1>
3. Add a second NTP server for redundancy: cluster time-service ntp server create -server <ntp2>
4. Verify time synchronization: cluster date show
5. If time is significantly off, manually set the time first: cluster date modify -dateandtime <date_time>
6. Best practice: Use at least 3 NTP servers for reliable time synchronization
7. Use internal NTP servers when available for security
support_case_note: Open a support case if time synchronization issues persist after NTP configuration, as this may indicate network-level NTP filtering.
kb_articles: KB-1015510: NTP configuration best practices for ONTAP

name: EMS Event Routing Not Configured
description: Detects clusters where EMS event notification routing may not be properly configured. Without event routing, critical alerts are only visible in the local EMS log and not forwarded to monitoring systems, email, or syslog servers.
check_query: SELECT system_id, node, severity, message_name, message_text, time FROM ontap_ems_events WHERE system_id = ? AND message_name LIKE '%ems.routing%' AND time >= datetime('now', '-168 hours') UNION ALL SELECT c.system_id, 'cluster' AS node, 'notice' AS severity, 'ems.check' AS message_name, 'EMS routing should be verified' AS message_text, datetime('now') AS time FROM ontap_clusters c WHERE c.system_id = ? AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ontap_ems_events e WHERE e.system_id = c.system_id AND e.message_name LIKE '%notifconfig%' AND e.time >= datetime('now', '-720 hours'))
condition: No evidence of EMS notification routing configuration in recent EMS events
impact: Critical events are not forwarded to monitoring systems. Alerts are delayed or missed entirely. Incident response time increases. SNMP traps are not sent. Syslog integration is not functional.
remediation_steps:
1. Check current event notification configuration: event notification show
2. Check event destinations: event notification destination show
3. Create syslog destination: event notification destination create -name syslog_dest -syslog <syslog_server>
4. Create email destination: event notification destination create -name email_dest -email <email_address>
5. Create SNMP destination: event notification destination create -name snmp_dest -snmp-traphost <trap_host>
6. Create notification filter: event notification create -filter-name important-events -destinations syslog_dest,email_dest
7. Configure important event filters: event filter create -filter-name critical-filter -rule-index 1 -type include -message-name * -severity EMERGENCY,ALERT,ERROR
support_case_note: No support case required.

name: Broadcast Domain Misconfiguration
description: Detects potential broadcast domain misconfigurations by identifying ports that are up but have no broadcast domain assignment, or ports in different broadcast domains that should be in the same one based on VLAN configuration.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, type, state, broadcast_domain, ipspace FROM ontap_ports WHERE system_id = ? AND state = 'up' AND (broadcast_domain IS NULL OR broadcast_domain = '')
condition: Active network port has no broadcast domain assignment
impact: LIFs cannot be created or failed over to ports without broadcast domain assignments. Network failover may not work correctly. Traffic may be sent to the wrong VLAN or subnet. Broadcast storms may occur with misconfigured domains.
remediation_steps:
1. Review broadcast domain assignments: network port broadcast-domain show
2. Check port membership: network port show -fields broadcast-domain
3. Add port to appropriate broadcast domain: network port broadcast-domain add-ports -broadcast-domain <domain> -ports <node:port>
4. If port is in wrong domain, remove and re-add: network port broadcast-domain remove-ports -broadcast-domain <old_domain> -ports <node:port>
5. Verify MTU consistency within broadcast domains: network port broadcast-domain show -fields mtu
6. Check for split broadcast domains: network port reachability show
7. Repair reachability if needed: network port reachability repair -node <node> -port <port>
support_case_note: Open a support case if broadcast domain repairs cause unexpected LIF migrations or if network connectivity issues persist after changes.

name: License Expired
description: Detects ONTAP feature licenses that have already expired. Expired licenses may cause features to stop functioning, potentially disrupting running workloads that depend on licensed features.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, state, expiry_date, compliance FROM ontap_licenses WHERE system_id = ? AND expiry_date IS NOT NULL AND expiry_date != '' AND expiry_date <= date('now')
condition: License expiry_date is on or before the current date
impact: Licensed features may stop functioning. SnapMirror replication may halt. FabricPool tiering may stop. FlexClone operations may be blocked. Encrypted volumes may become inaccessible. Feature-dependent workflows will fail.
remediation_steps:
1. Check license status: license show -fields package,type,expiration
2. Identify expired licenses: license show -fields expiration -expiration <=now
3. Generate replacement license keys: Visit https://mysupport.netapp.com/site/tools/tool-eula/license-generator
4. Install new license: license add -license-code <key>
5. Verify license is active: license show -package <feature>
6. If using ONTAP 9.10.1+, check license manager status: system license show-status
7. Contact your NetApp account team for license renewal
8. For emergency license keys, contact NetApp Support
support_case_note: Open a P2 support case for emergency license keys if features are currently disrupted. Provide the cluster serial number.

name: License Approaching Expiry
description: Detects ONTAP feature licenses that will expire within the next 90 days. Early detection allows proactive renewal to prevent feature disruptions.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, state, expiry_date, compliance FROM ontap_licenses WHERE system_id = ? AND expiry_date IS NOT NULL AND expiry_date != '' AND expiry_date > date('now') AND expiry_date <= date('now', '+90 days')
condition: License expiry_date is within the next 90 days
impact: Licensed features will stop functioning when the license expires. Planning time for renewal is limited. Budget approval for license renewal may be needed. Procurement lead times may cause gaps in coverage.
remediation_steps:
1. Review expiring licenses: license show -fields package,type,expiration
2. Contact your NetApp account team for renewal quotes
3. Generate replacement license keys before expiry: Visit https://mysupport.netapp.com/site/tools/tool-eula/license-generator
4. Plan license installation during a change window
5. Install new license: license add -license-code <key>
6. Verify new expiration date: license show -package <feature> -fields expiration
7. Set a calendar reminder 30 days before the new expiry date
8. Consider converting to perpetual licenses to avoid future expiry concerns
support_case_note: No support case required for license renewal. Contact your NetApp sales team or use the License Generator on the NetApp Support site.

name: Feature License Non-Compliant
description: Detects ONTAP features that are in use without a valid or compliant license. Non-compliant licenses indicate that a feature is being used beyond its entitlement, which can lead to feature restrictions or legal/contractual issues.
check_query: SELECT system_id, name, state, compliance, expiry_date FROM ontap_licenses WHERE system_id = ? AND compliance IS NOT NULL AND compliance != '' AND compliance != 'compliant' AND state != 'active'
condition: License compliance is not 'compliant' and state is not 'active'
impact: Features may be forcibly disabled. Entitlement overage may result in additional charges. Legal/contractual obligations are not being met. NetApp Support may decline to assist with unlicensed features.
remediation_steps:
1. Check license compliance: license show -fields package,state,compliance
2. Identify non-compliant features: license show -fields compliance -compliance !compliant
3. Determine usage: license show -fields capacity-used,capacity-limit
4. If over capacity, either reduce usage or purchase additional capacity
5. Install corrected licenses: license add -license-code <key>
6. Verify compliance: license show -fields compliance
7. Contact NetApp account team for license true-up if needed
8. For ONTAP 9.10.1+: system license show-status
support_case_note: Contact your NetApp account team for license compliance resolution. Open a support case if features are being restricted due to compliance issues.

name: Cloud Licensing Disconnected
description: Detects potential Cloud ONTAP or capacity-based licensing connectivity issues. Cloud-based licensing requires periodic phone-home to the NetApp License Manager. A disconnection may result in a grace period expiry and feature lockout.
check_query: SELECT system_id, node, severity, message_name, message_text, time FROM ontap_ems_events WHERE system_id = ? AND (message_name LIKE '%license.cloud%' OR message_name LIKE '%license.manager%' OR message_name LIKE '%license.phone%' OR message_name LIKE '%nlm%') AND severity IN ('warning', 'error', 'alert') AND time >= datetime('now', '-168 hours')
condition: EMS events indicate cloud licensing connectivity issues within the last 7 days
impact: Grace period countdown begins when license manager connectivity is lost. If grace period expires, licensed features may be disabled. Cloud Volumes ONTAP may stop serving data. Capacity-based licenses may not report accurate usage.
remediation_steps:
1. Check license manager status: system license show-status (ONTAP 9.10.1+)
2. Verify network connectivity to NetApp license servers: network ping -destination <license_server>
3. Check DNS resolution: dns check -vserver <admin_svm>
4. Check proxy configuration if applicable: system node autosupport show -fields proxy-url
5. Verify firewall rules allow outbound HTTPS to NetApp license servers
6. If connectivity cannot be restored, contact NetApp for offline license activation
7. Monitor grace period remaining: system license show-status -fields grace-period
8. Ensure intercluster LIFs have internet access for license verification
support_case_note: Open a support case if cloud licensing connectivity cannot be restored within the grace period. NetApp can provide temporary offline licensing to prevent feature lockout.
